windows.transcript: Filtering ensures purity, removing every trace of impurity.
windows.old_description: Molten wax processed in the vat.
windows.embed_text_v2: Straining and filtering molten golden-amber candle wax through a conical fine-mesh metal sieve, thick stream then thin drips falling into an amber wax bath, large glowing stainless-steel factory tanks.
windows.summary_v2: Filtered molten amber wax drains through a conical fine-mesh strainer into a vat in a candle factory, then cuts to wide shot of glowing stainless-steel wax tanks.

tags_v2.action: filtering molten wax through mesh strainer

seconds_v2.desc: Macro shot of a conical fine-mesh metal strainer holding pooled molten golden-amber wax, with a thick translucent stream draining down through its tip into an amber wax bath below.
The strained wax continues pouring in a thick glossy ribbon from the underside of the mesh cone down into the liquid wax surface beneath.
Stream thins slightly as filtered wax falls into the vat, small droplets splashing off and ripples beginning to spread across the molten amber pool.
Pour narrows to a single thin twisting strand dripping from the cone's tip, droplets clinging to the lower mesh rim, concentric ripples forming on the wax surface.
A slow elongated droplet stretches and falls from the strainer tip into the wax bath, sending a clean circular ripple outward across the glassy amber surface.
Thin steady drip resumes from the mesh cone, beads of wax held along the rim, the dripping point disturbing the center of the rippling molten pool.
Three thin parallel strands of filtered wax run from the underside of the strainer down into the vat, breaking the surface and spreading rings outward.
Multiple fine wax streams and falling droplets descend from the mesh cone into the amber bath, concentric ripples radiating across the molten wax surface.
Cut to a wide view of a stainless-steel processing facility with tall cylindrical tanks, their inspection windows glowing bright orange from the hot molten wax inside.
